mappings.rationaleAI: Education systems examined through philosophical and social perspectives address philosophies of education (A). Teaching methods and pedagogy address the teaching art (B, B.2). Institutional forms and educational inequality address the organization of education (C).

Higher education institutions from medieval origins to contemporary challenges directly address colleges, universities, and professional schools (C.1.c). Tuition fees and public funding address the economics of education (C.3). Digital and distance learning address instructional media (B.2.d).
Psychological science of learning through associative theories of conditioning directly addresses processes of learning and thinking including experimental findings and theories (B.1).
Developmental psychology covering cognitive development, language acquisition, and brain development underlying learning directly addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Cognitive psychology of thinking, reasoning, problem solving, and decision making directly addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Human intelligence covering psychometric measurement, cognitive abilities, and the Flynn effect directly addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Memory as a cognitive phenomenon covering encoding, storage, retrieval, and the relationship between sleep and memory consolidation addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).

Psychological perspectives on creativity covering models of creative expression and the relationship between individual genius and cultural environment address processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Psychology covering cognitive psychology (perception, attention, memory, intelligence) and developmental psychology provides foundations for understanding processes of learning and thinking (B.1).

Developmental psychology of adolescence covering cognitive maturation and identity formation addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1). The adolescent life stage as a transition connects to elementary and secondary education contexts (C.1.b).

Autism spectrum disorder covering social communication difficulties, restricted interests, and diagnostic frameworks directly addresses special education and the education of exceptional children (C.1.d).
Dyslexia as a reading disorder covering diagnosis, interventions, and evidence-based strategies directly addresses special education and the education of exceptional children (C.1.d).

Historical survey of reading and literacy covering the evolution from elite oral reading to mass literacy addresses the art and science of teaching (B.2). Public education and national literacy levels connect to education of the adult population (C.1.e).
Psycholinguistics studying how humans process, comprehend, produce, and learn language addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
AI applications in understanding memory, learning, and language processing connect to instructional media including computer-based instruction (B.2.d).
Information theory covering computation, digital technologies, and the Internet addresses instructional media including computer-based instruction (B.2.d).
Human resource management covering workforce strategy, employee training, and skills development directly addresses vocational training, apprenticeship, and employee training (C.1.f).
Global historical survey of childhood covering changing conceptions of children's welfare and reform movements provides context for the organization of education (C) across cultures and periods.
Economics covering human capital formation and development outcomes addresses the economics of education (C.3).
